terms.definition: A special day or time when people celebrate together with joy, food, music, and prayers.
terms.exampleUsage: Diwali is a festival of lights celebrated all over India.

terms.definition: A chart that shows all the days, weeks, and months of a year. It helps us keep track of dates.
terms.exampleUsage: I marked my birthday on the calendar.

terms.definition: The time when farmers gather their ripe crops from the fields.
terms.exampleUsage: After the harvest, farmers celebrated with a feast.

terms.definition: A part of the year with a particular type of weather. India has summer, winter, monsoon, spring, and autumn.
terms.exampleUsage: Winter is the season when we wear sweaters and shawls.

terms.definition: A custom or belief that is passed down from parents and grandparents over many years.
terms.exampleUsage: It is our family tradition to make sweets during Diwali.

terms.definition: A national holiday on 26th January when India celebrates the day its Constitution came into effect in 1950.
terms.exampleUsage: We watched the Republic Day parade on television.

terms.definition: A harvest festival celebrated in January when people fly kites and eat sweets made from sesame seeds and jaggery.
terms.exampleUsage: We flew kites on Makar Sankranti.

terms.definition: A large fair with stalls, games, food, and fun activities, often held during festivals.
terms.exampleUsage: We went to a mela and enjoyed the rides and cotton candy.

terms.definition: A national holiday on 15th August celebrating India's freedom from British rule in 1947.
terms.exampleUsage: We sang the national anthem on Independence Day.

terms.definition: A small oil lamp made of clay, lit during festivals like Diwali.
terms.exampleUsage: We placed diyas around the house to light it up on Diwali.
